summary: A lightweight, stateless Python library providing email validation and US phone number formatting using only the standard library. Designed for embedding in larger applications with minimal dependencies, high reliability, and predictable performance.

components.name: EmailValidator
components.responsibility: Validate email addresses using regex-based pattern matching and structural checks (presence of '@', domain validity, character restrictions).

components.name: PhoneNumberFormatter
components.responsibility: Normalize and format phone numbers by stripping non-digit characters and applying US (XXX) XXX-XXXX pattern; handles optional country codes by truncating to last 10 digits.

components.name: ValidationService
components.responsibility: Orchestrates validation and formatting logic, applies edge-case handling (empty/null input), and returns consistent responses with error resilience.

deployment_strategy: Package distribution via PyPI or direct import as a module; no runtime deployment required. Versioned using semantic versioning. Distributed as source-only wheel.
security_considerations: Input sanitization built into validation logic prevents injection via malformed strings
No external I/O, network calls, or filesystem access reduces attack surface
All inputs are type-checked and defensively handled to avoid crashes or information leakage
scalability_notes: Stateless and thread-safe; scales horizontally by usage in concurrent contexts
O(1) time complexity for both operations ensures sub-10ms latency even under load
Extensible via subclassing or composition — new validators/formatters can be added without modifying core classes
